item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I really enjoyed the read. I liked that you created a skip that allowed for you to write several types of stories while still maintaining a professional write up. I have seen more than a few entries use blue text to show updates to the log, but I believe this is the first time I have seen all these different colors being used. It works very well, is creative, and exceedingly unique among SCP entries.

Some points I think could be worked on:

  1. test logs for all the pens
  2. more information for the black pen. The [DATA EXPUNGED] does make sense in context, but perhaps you could have a code that says "Enter 05 credentials" and provide small details.
  3. The interview could be improved a bit. As Damian Uck pointed out, if the pens were pretty simple to make, why not make another set?

All in all, a strongly unique entry, with some room to be fleshed out. It's a +1 from me.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Although I like the concept, the unnecessary [DATA EXPUNGED] with the black pen rubs me in the wrong way for the skip in totality. I feel like you could cut the black pen and just have SCP-4250-1 through 5, and the skip wouldn't really change.

Novote.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
As a bisexual man, I have to agree with the D-class. That elf man and human man romance novel did sound like a gay-ass book.
Loved the skip by the way.
+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I'm with Zedbrains on this one. Killing one live or exposing the masses to something that was produced by an anomaly, it's a simple calculation which one is the lesser evil

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
If she has bills to pay, why doesn't she just make another set of pens and write a story.
She said it's quite simple apparently, just write a bestseller.

Then again… Are We Cool Yet have the underlying philosophy about their anart being art. Not meant as a cash cow they can milk whenever they need some cash.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I don't know, I disagree. The foundation has to be extra, extra careful with any SCP considering they're dealing with the anomalous and may not know everything about it, or may even be wrong about something specific.
Exposing the general public at large to something created by an SCP I think would be considered far too risky and worth the life of a D-Class

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Having a test guaranteed to end in the death of a D-class no matter what would probably be shut down by the Ethics Committee. The Foundation attempts to protect human life where possible, including that of the D-class. The risk of the only awcy member that could recognize the anomaly was not known to the foundation, and was dealt with anyway. It is a waste of resources to expend the lives of test subjects when an alternative easily presents itself.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Yeah I understood that but why does the foundation care? D-Class are intended to be expendable, it would be a lot less effort and present less of a threat of containment breach (as actually happened in the article) if the affected subject was just killed, Right?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Part of me wants one of those pens for NaNoWriMo
+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Part of the compulsion the pens cause is to publish; only once the work is published the anomalous behavior ends.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Why does the foundation have to publish anything the pens write?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Thanks, I'll try that one out instead.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: The color you use for the yellow is hard to read on the grey of the collapsible blocks. Might I recommend #FFD632, for a more legible and easy to read color, while still being yellow?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Thanks, lol. I just like having fun and helping people out.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
You're so great!!! Thanks for letting other writers use your codes, I'm not a good coder at all and this helps a lot!

You're like the Jonas Salk of wikidot bb you have my deepest gratitude

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Any code I make is free to copy. I've also recently updated the boxes to look nicer if you'd prefer to use them instead.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Yeah I really loved ur article and your amazing format so I copied the source codes for that drop down box I hope that's okay!!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Yo no way, that's my interview log format thing. Awesome.

also, pilot pens are amazing

item.title: Another SCP of mine
item.content:encoded:
This is now the 8th SCP I've written, and (hopefully) the 4th that will stay up on this website. This SCP took me a bit longer than usual to write, as I've been busy with college, but I'm glad to announce 4250! I'd like to thank all of you once again, and please, leave feedback! I'm always looking for ways to improve!

Also, the pens are my own, as are the samples, so there's no issues of copyright there. Feel free to read this article with the assurance that I have not infringed on any content in crafting this SCP for you all to read!
